title: Daily Summary — 2026-05-02
body:
## Recent Activity

Synced window: 2026-05-01T11:37:55Z to 2026-05-02T11:37:55Z. Signals show 1 issue, 3 PRs, 1 discussion, and 0 commits updated.

Activity centered on child/delegated orchestration design, review/fix loops for PR #77, rubric-route naming and scope discussion on PR #15, and project-management label summaries.

## Recently Active PRs

- Open: [#77 Add delegate_issue child orchestration](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/77) had the most activity. Reviews still flagged NEEDS_REWORK around trusted marker provenance, stale orchestrator docs, and terminal-state classification; a follow-up orchestrated fix-pr run reported no code changes.
- Open: [#66 Support child issue sub-orchestrators](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/66) continued design discussion, with the newer `delegate_issue` direction tracked via [#76](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/76) and PR #77.
- Open: [#15 Add minimal add-rubrics route with dedicated workflow](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/15) received discussion about whether the route should remain `/add-rubrics`; the response favored keeping the name to avoid confusion with existing rubrics update automation.

## Recently Active Issues

- Open: [#79 Better capture orchestrated task error](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/79) was opened for an orchestration reliability failure where a failed task stopped subsequent orchestration. It is labeled `priority/p1` and `effort/medium`.

## Recently Active Discussions

- [#78 Daily Summary — 2026-05-01](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/78) was updated with project-management summaries. The latest summary elevated issue #79 and kept PR #77 near the top of the triage queue.

## Follow-ups

1. Fix or re-run PR #77 follow-up work for the remaining NEEDS_REWORK items, especially marker author/source validation.
2. Use #79 to capture the failed-orchestration behavior from PR #77 and make the failure path actionable.
3. Decide whether PR #66 should be closed, superseded, or kept only as design context now that PR #77 carries the cleaner direction.

---
*Generated by the agent-daily-summary workflow*

## Project Management Summary

- Mode: `labels applied`
- Open items assessed: `26 issues, 4 pull requests`
- Managed labels: `priority/*`, `effort/*`

### Top Triage Queue

- `issue#79` Better capture orchestrated task error — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` — orchestration failures can silently stop downstream coordination; recent and operationally important. Label changes: none needed.
- `issue#75` Agent review-body commands can be cancelled by sibling inline review-comment events — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` — nondeterministic command loss in a common PR review path; bounded workflow fix. Label changes: none needed.
- `issue#72` Adding timeout for agent tasks — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — needed to prevent hung agent runs and keep orchestrator reporting reliable; cross-cutting runtime/workflow work. Label changes: none needed.
- `issue#70` Codex review lane can hang in acpx and block synthesis — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` — active reliability bug that blocks review synthesis; related to timeout hardening. Label changes: none needed.
- `pull_request#77` Add delegate_issue child orchestration — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — active draft for major orchestration architecture, closes high-impact issue #76, with recent review/fix activity. Label changes: none needed.
- `issue#51` Orchestrator to dispatch to another orchestrator — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — major orchestration capability and continuation design, tied to staged/complex work. Label changes: none needed.
- `issue#26` Automatically report issues on GitHub — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — important reliability feedback loop for failed automations; broad discussion/reporting workflow scope. Label changes: none needed.
- `pull_request#62` Add first-run onboarding setup workflow — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — draft PR for high-impact installation/onboarding issue #23. Label changes: none needed.
- `pull_request#15` Add minimal add-rubrics route with dedicated workflow — `priority/p2`, `effort/high` — valuable feature PR with remaining draft follow-ups, but not as urgent as reliability/orchestration blockers. Label changes: none needed.
- `issue#65` Refactor generated comment minimization helpers — `priority/p2`, `effort/high` — umbrella refactor for comment minimization behavior across reviews and fix-pr output. Label changes: none needed.

### Label Changes

No managed label changes were needed. All assessed open issues and pull requests already had exactly one selected priority label and one selected effort label consistent with current triage.


### Notes

No items were skipped. GitHub issue and PR metadata was available after retrying with network access. The local checkout only contained top-level repository files, so triage relied primarily on GitHub issue/PR titles, labels, recency, and visible discussion context.

- Applied 0 managed priority/effort label operation(s).

## Project Management Summary

### Top Triage Queue

- `pull_request#85` Allow delegate_issue to adopt existing issues — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` — Active PR unblocking orchestrator reuse of existing issues for #83; recent review/fix loop shows remaining minor follow-up but the branch is highly actionable. Planned label changes: add `priority/p1`, `effort/medium`.
- `issue#84` Preserve orchestrator handoff context for resumed fix-pr runs — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` — Directly affects orchestrator correctness by causing actionable `fix-pr` handoffs to become false no-op runs. Planned label changes: none.
- `issue#79` Handle orchestrated task no_changes/failure visibly and recover when safe — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` — Important orchestration reliability issue with clear acceptance criteria and recent related failures. Planned label changes: none.
- `issue#75` Agent review-body commands can be cancelled by sibling inline review-comment events — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` — User-facing trigger reliability bug that can silently drop valid agent commands. Planned label changes: none.
- `issue#83` Orchestrator robustness fixes — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — Parent/tracking issue for #84 and #79; currently blocked on orchestrator child/adoption flow and should stay high in the queue. Planned label changes: add `priority/p1`, `effort/high`.
- `issue#72` Adding timeout for agent tasks — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — Timeouts are needed for visible failure reporting and orchestrator flow control, with cross-cutting runtime/workflow changes. Planned label changes: none.
- `issue#70` Codex review lane can hang in acpx and block synthesis — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` — Review lane hangs can block synthesis and automation progress; scoped but operationally important. Planned label changes: none.
- `issue#51` Orchestrator to dispatch to another orchestrator — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — Strategic orchestrator capability for multi-stage work, with substantial architecture and coordination implications. Planned label changes: none.
- `issue#43` Allow /orchestrate follow-up patches from merged or closed PRs — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — Important workflow gap for post-merge follow-up work; likely cross-route behavior change. Planned label changes: none.
- `issue#26` Automatically report issues on GitHub — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — High-impact reliability/visibility feature for failed agent runs, but broad and policy-sensitive. Planned label changes: none.

### Notes

No local project-plan references were found in the checkout. GitHub API access was initially blocked by the sandbox, then the read-only issue/PR queries succeeded with escalation. Existing managed labels were left unchanged where they already matched the current priority/effort assessment.

### Managed Label Application

- Applied 10 managed priority/effort label operation(s).

Not end-to-end today.

Discussion mentions work for routes like `/answer`, and discussions/comments are recognized trigger surfaces in the docs: [`supported-workflows.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md#L140-L156). But the actual `/orchestrate` router job is gated to only `issue` or `pull_request` targets: [`agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml#L655-L662). The orchestrator workflow inputs/docs also describe the target as an issue or PR, not a discussion: [`agent-orchestrator.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml#L16-L21), [`agent-orchestrator.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md#L39-L46).

So: use `/orchestrate` from an issue or PR. From a discussion, orchestration would need a bridge that creates/adopts a tracking issue, then starts the orchestrator there.
